title: Handle multi-part RAR archives as one model (.partN.rar volumes)
description:
Multi-volume RAR sets (name.part1.rar/name.part01.rar ... name.partN.rar) are walked as independent files: the first volume extracts the whole set (tagged), while continuation volumes extract partial/garbage content — sometimes 'tagged' with wrong data, sometimes 'no valid content' errors. A 7-part 'Krieg' set becomes up to 7 separate models. Observed 33 .partN.rar rows incl. continuations (Krieg.part03, Chaos Warlord.part02, ...).

Fix: treat a .partN.rar set as ONE model. unrar pulls the whole set from the first volume (siblings live alongside in --zips), so: (1) tag discovery skips continuation volumes (part number != 1); (2) strip the .partN marker from derived names so the model is 'Krieg' not 'Krieg part01' (in utils.clean_file_name + manyfold_ingest._model_dir_name); (3) run_upload skips continuation-volume rows defensively; (4) the tag resume drops stale continuation rows so existing CSVs self-heal. Add utils helpers multipart_volume_number / strip_multipart_suffix. Note: 'Tyranid Warriors Part 3.rar' (space, single .rar) is NOT a volume and must be unaffected.

Follow-up (separate): --delete-source should remove all sibling volumes of a set, not just the first.
close_reason: Implemented: multi-part RAR sets handled as one model (first volume only, .partN stripped, continuations skipped in tag+upload, resume self-heals)

title: _flatten_into_root: cap flattened filename length to avoid ENAMETOOLONG
description: _flatten_into_root names each flattened file '_'.join(rel.parts), i.e. the full nested path joined by underscores. For deeply nested archives this exceeds the filesystem 255-byte per-component limit and raises [Errno 63] File name too long, so the whole archive fails to stage. Observed: General/Chubby Pattern Contender... produced a 200+ char name. Fix: when the joined name exceeds a safe length (~200 bytes), shorten it while preserving the suffix and keeping uniqueness — e.g. truncate the joined stem and append a short hash of the relative path. Add a test with a deeply nested member whose joined name exceeds the limit, asserting the staged file exists with a capped, unique name and the suffix preserved.
close_reason: Implemented in PR #45: client re-auths on 401, flatten caps overlong names

title: ManyfoldClient: refresh OAuth token on 401 (long runs expire the cached token)
description: _ensure_token caches self._token and never refreshes; _request retries only on 429/5xx, not 401. Manyfold's OAuth access token (Doorkeeper, ~2h default TTL) expires during long runs, so all API calls after expiry fail with 'HTTP 401: Failed to Login'. Observed on a 4h49m full upload: every matched-model get_model/update after ~2.5h failed (General/Execrator, General/Cato Sicarius, Finished scans/Execrator, Finished scans/Cato Sicarius). Fix: in _request, on a 401 response, clear self._token, call _ensure_token() to re-auth, and retry the request once. Add a test: first call 401s, client re-auths and retries, second succeeds.
close_reason: Implemented in PR #45: client re-auths on 401, flatten caps overlong names

title: stage_into_library: flatten archive contents so Manyfold creates one model per zip
description:
Manyfold treats each folder containing 3D files as a single model and each SUBFOLDER as a separate model (confirmed via Manyfold docs/maintainer: manyfold.app/manual/scanning.html, discussions/5020 — no setting controls this; only a manual merge feature exists). stage_into_library extracts each archive into a folder with internal subfolders (Supported/, Large Printers/, Body/, ...), so a single zip fans out into many models, none of which receive our datapackage.json tags (they end up with only Manyfold's '!new' auto-tag). Observed: a 257-row run produced 826+ untagged, uncollected models.

Fix: in stage_into_library, after extraction, flatten every file under the staging dir into the staging root (no subfolders) with collision-safe names derived from the file's relative path (separators -> '_'), then write datapackage.json at the root. Manyfold then sees one folder = one model with all parts at its root, and the datapackage tags apply.

Add a test (mock patoolib.extract_archive to write a nested tree incl. a same-basename collision across subfolders) asserting the staged dest is flat (no subdirs), parts are present with collision-safe names, the root-level files are preserved, and datapackage.json sits at the root with the tags.

Caveat for rollout: existing staged folders on disk short-circuit via 'if dest.exists()'; to re-stage flat, the old folders in MANYFOLD_LIBRARY_PATH must be removed (user is deleting the bad models manually).
close_reason: Implemented: stage_into_library flattens extracted archive contents into one flat folder so Manyfold creates one model per zip

title: Recursive --zips: tag + upload must handle nested folders
description:
Both tag and upload assume model archives sit directly in --zips, so a nested
incoming library is mostly missed.

- tag: src/tagging.py uses Path(zips_dir).iterdir() (non-recursive) — archives
  in subfolders are never tagged.
- upload: src/manyfold_ingest.py uses source = Path(zips_dir) / filename (flat),
  and the CSV stores only path.name (basename), so nested sources aren't found
  and same-basename files in different subfolders collide on one CSV row.

Fix: tag recurses with rglob and writes the path RELATIVE to --zips as the CSV
filename (recursive + collision-free); upload joins that relative path onto
--zips to locate the source. match_model and _model_dir_name already use
Path(...).stem/.name (final component), so naming/matching keep working with a
relative-path filename. Add tests for nested discovery, relative-path CSV, and
collision avoidance.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #41: tag recurses with rglob and writes relative-path CSV filenames; upload re-joins onto --zips

Support all Manyfold file formats (3D mesh, slicer/chitubox, images) in tag + upload
description: The pipeline only recognizes .stl/.obj/.png (+ .zip/.rar/.7z archives), so archives/loose files in formats Manyfold supports (e.g. .chitubox/.ctb/.lys slicer projects, .3mf/.ply/.step meshes) are skipped as 'no valid content' and never tagged or staged. Match Manyfold's supported set (manyfold.app/manual/supported_formats.html). Centralize the extension lists in utils.py (MESH/SLICER/IMAGE/ARCHIVE/MODEL/LOOSE sets) as one source of truth, and use them in: tagging.extract_to_temp (archive vs loose copy), is_valid_archive_content (allowed exts), the discovery loop, and manyfold_ingest _ARCHIVE_EXTS/_LOOSE_EXTS. Keep the executable bad_exts security gate. Loose documents/video/PCB are NOT treated as standalone models (but already ride along inside archives via flatten). Update README formats list. Add tests for new mesh + slicer formats (incl. a chitubox-only archive now tagging).
close_reason: Implemented: centralized Manyfold's supported formats in utils (mesh/slicer/image/archive) and used across tag + upload

title: run_upload: same-basename files in different --zips subfolders collide on one staged folder
description:
Since recursive --zips (relative-path CSV filenames), two different sources sharing a basename (e.g. 'General/Triarch Praetorians.zip' and 'Finished scans/Triarch Praetorians.zip') both stage to library/_model_dir_name(basename) = 'Triarch Praetorians'. The second hits 'if dest.exists(): return dest' and is silently NOT staged — its model never reaches Manyfold. Observed: 5 such pairs in a 257-row run (General/ vs Finished scans/).

Fix: in run_upload, pre-scan CSV filenames and assign each a unique staged-folder name; when a basename collides across rows, disambiguate with the relative parent path (e.g. 'Triarch Praetorians (General)' vs 'Triarch Praetorians (Finished scans)'). Pass the resolved dest name into stage_into_library (new optional dest_name param). Non-colliding names stay clean. This stages BOTH models; each still gets its tags from its own datapackage at scan time.

Known residual (document, don't fix here): on the second upload pass, faction-collection matching is basename-slug based, so same-basename models may match ambiguously — but both are staged and tagged via datapackage regardless.

Tests: _staged_dir_names disambiguates collisions / keeps non-colliding clean; run_upload stages two same-basename sources into two distinct folders.
close_reason: Implemented: _staged_dir_names disambiguates same-basename sources by relative parent so both stage into distinct folders (folded into PR #45)

title: _model_dir_name doesn't dedupe repeated words, producing doubled model titles
description:
Vendor 'doubled name' filenames (e.g. 'Sister Superior_Sister+Superior.zip') produce a doubled model title/folder when staged into Manyfold: _model_dir_name (src/manyfold_ingest.py:98) yields 'Sister Superior Sister Superior', which becomes both the staged folder name and the datapackage.json title/name. Surfaced in Manyfold as a model titled/slugged 'sister-superior-sister-superior'. normalize_name already dedupes repeated words via filter_query_tokens (so match_model works), but _model_dir_name only does regex cleanup with no dedupe, so the human-facing title/folder is doubled.

Fix: dedupe repeated words case-insensitively in _model_dir_name (preserving order and original casing, mirroring filter_query_tokens' seen-set), without dropping digits/apostrophes that the title legitimately needs.

Add a test for the doubled-name -> single-title case (e.g. 'Sister Superior_Sister+Superior.zip' -> 'Sister Superior'), and confirm normal/non-doubled names are unaffected.

Note: this is distinct from the Manyfold 'Path does not match template' problems, which are Manyfold's path-template/Organize-files feature reacting to our flat staged folder vs a tag-nested template -- not a bug in our upload.
close_reason: Fixed: _model_dir_name dedupes repeated words case-insensitively (combined PR)

title: Add tagged_at timestamp column to tagging CSV
description: Record when each file was tagged. Append a tagged_at column (ISO-8601 UTC) to the tagging CSV header and write the current time for each newly-tagged/retried row. Kept rows on resume retain their original timestamp. Resume logic must migrate older CSVs that predate the column by backfilling an empty timestamp instead of forcing a fresh file. Upload reads via DictReader so the extra column is ignored.
close_reason: Implemented in PR #42: trailing tagged_at ISO-8601 UTC column with in-place migration of pre-timestamp CSVs

Log retrieval distance + weak-context flag to tagging.log for diagnosability
description:
When a file gets sparse tags (e.g. a blank faction), we currently can't tell from the logs WHY — we have to infer it from token counts and the slug math. Aveline.stl came back with no faction while 'Aveline Plasma no Shield.stl' did, and the only available explanation was inferred (single-token query -> likely unfiltered fallback past the 0.8 weak-context threshold -> model told to ignore lore -> declined faction), not recorded.

Make this diagnosable instead of inferred: in run_tagging (src/tagging.py), include per-file retrieval diagnostics in the tagging.log 'Tagged ...' line (and/or skip lines), specifically:
- best-match distance (distances[0])
- whether the retrieval was slug-filtered vs unfiltered fallback (slug_filtered)
- whether the weak-context note fired (the _WEAK_CONTEXT_DISTANCE guard on the unfiltered path)

So a future 'why did X get no faction?' is answerable straight from tagging.log. Small, log-only change; add a test asserting the new fields appear (the weak-context-fired case is already exercised by test_structured.py).
close_reason: Implemented: tagging.log Tagged line now records dist/slug_filtered/weak_context (combined PR)
